Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider a household water heater. Let us say that we would like to determine how much heat we must transfer to the water in the tank in order to supply a steady stream of hot water. For such an analysis, the water heater should be considered as

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Since hot water will leave the tank and be replaced by cold water, it is not convenient to choose a fixed mass (closed system) as our system for analysis. Instead, we can concentrate our attention to the volume formed by the interior surfaces of the tank and consider the cold and hot water streams as mass leaving and entering the control volume. Hence, the approach of an open system should be used.

(d)

Consider a simple compressible system in which there are no electrical, magnetic, gravitational, motion and surface tension effects. The state of such a system can be completely specified by number of independent, intensive properties where is ______. (Answer in integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2 (2 - 2)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider 2 kg water in a pan is being heated on a gas stove. The heating element of the stove supplies 15 kJ of thermal energy to the water. Assuming that 3 kJ of this energy is lost from water to surrounding air and specific heat of water is 4.2 J/g°C, the change in temperature (in K) of water in the pan is ______.

(Round off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.4
Solution

1.4 (1.4 - 1.4)

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2 (2 - 2)

Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding a quasi-equilibrium process are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c, d)
Work-producing devices deliver the most work when they operate on quasi-equilibrium process.
While, work-consuming devices require the least work when they operate on quasi-equilibrium processes.

[Assume, Patm = 100 kPa and g = 9.8 m/s2]

Correct answer: D

Q12 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a system as shown below with volume A containing an ideal gas initially while the volume B is evacuated initially. The system is under equilibrium at this state. Now the valve is opened. Which of the following will be observed at this stage?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
This is one of the classical experiments of thermodynamics, which was first performed by Joule. Obviously, after opening the valve, the pressure and volume of the ideal gas will change markedly, but the temperature does not change because there is no change in temperature, there is no net heat transfer to the water. This experiment concludes that since no work is done, from the first law, the internal energy of an ideal gas does not depend on pressure or volume.

NAT
Correct answer: 7692
Solution

7692 (7692 - 7693)

NAT
Correct answer: 69.86

NAT
Correct answer: 10.7
Solution

Q17 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are not example(s) of pure substances?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)
• Air, even being a mixture of several gases, is considered as a pure substance because it has a uniform composition.
• A mixture of liquid air and gaseous air, however, is not a pure substance since the composition of liquid air is different from the composition of gaseous air, and thus the mixture is no longer chemically homogenous.
• A mixture of oil and water is not a pure substance. Since oil is not soluble in water, it will collect on top of the water, forming two chemically dissimilar regions.
• A mixture of ice and liquid water is a pure substance because both phases have the same chemical composition.
